H5401.derivation: a primitive root (identical with H5400 (נָשַׂק), through the idea of fastening up; compare H2388 (חָזַק), H2836 (חָשַׁק));
H5401.strongs_def: to kiss, literally or figuratively (touch); also (as a mode of attachment), to equip with weapons

H5402.strongs_def: military equipment, i.e. (collectively) arms (offensive or defensive), or (concretely) an arsenal
H5402.kjv_def: armed men, armour(-y), battle, harness, weapon.
H5402.usage: armour(3x), weapons(3x), battle(1x), armed(1x), harness(1x), armoury(1x)

H5404.derivation: from an unused root meaning to lacerate;
H5404.strongs_def: the eagle (or other large bird of prey)

H5405.strongs_def: properly, to eliminate, i.e. (intransitively) to dry up

H5408.kjv_def: cut (in pieces), divide, hew in pieces.

H5410.derivation: or (feminine) נְתִיבָה; or נְתִבָה; (Jeremiah 6:16), from an unused root meaning to tramp;

H5411.derivation: or נָתוּן (Ezra 8:17), (the proper form as passive participle), from H5414 (נָתַן); one given, i.e. (in the plural only)
H5411.strongs_def: the Nethinim, or Temple-servants (as given to that duty)

H5412.strongs_def: {the Nethinim, or Temple-servants (as given to that duty)}

H5413.strongs_def: to flow forth (literally or figuratively); by implication, to liquify

H5414.strongs_def: to give, used with greatest latitude of application (put, make, etc.)
H5414.kjv_def: add, apply, appoint, ascribe, assign, [idiom] avenge, [idiom] be (healed), bestow, bring (forth, hither), cast, cause, charge, come, commit, consider, count, [phrase] cry, deliver (up), direct, distribute, do, [idiom] doubtless, [idiom] without fail, fasten, frame, [idiom] get, give (forth, over, up), grant, hang (up), [idiom] have, [idiom] indeed, lay (unto charge, up), (give) leave, lend, let (out), [phrase] lie, lift up, make, [phrase] O that, occupy, offer, ordain, pay, perform, place, pour, print, [idiom] pull, put (forth), recompense, render, requite, restore, send (out), set (forth), shew, shoot forth (up), [phrase] sing, [phrase] slander, strike, (sub-) mit, suffer, [idiom] surely, [idiom] take, thrust, trade, turn, utter, [phrase] weep, [phrase] willingly, [phrase] withdraw, [phrase] would (to) God, yield.
H5414.usage: put(191x), deliver(174x), made(107x), set(99x), up(26x), lay(22x), grant(21x), suffer(18x), yield(15x), bring(15x), cause(13x), utter(12x), laid(11x), send(11x), recompense(11x), appoint(10x), shew(7x)

H5416.strongs_def: Nathan, the name of five Israelites

H5417.derivation: from H5414 (נָתַן) and H410 (אֵל); given of God;
H5417.strongs_def: Nethanel, the name of ten Israelites

H5418.derivation: or נְתַנְיָהוּ; from H5414 (נָתַן) and H3050 (יָהּ); given of Jah;
H5418.strongs_def: Nethanjah, the name of four Israelites

H5419.derivation: from H5414 (נָתַן) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); given of (the) king;

H5422.kjv_def: beat down, break down (out), cast down, destroy, overthrow, pull down, throw down.

H5423.kjv_def: break (off), burst, draw (away), lift up, pluck (away, off), pull (out), root out.

H5425.strongs_def: to jump, i.e. be violently agitated; causatively, to terrify, shake off, untie
H5425.kjv_def: drive asunder, leap, (let) loose, [idiom] make, move, undo.

H5426.strongs_def: {to jump, i.e. be violently agitated; causatively, to terrify, shake off, untie}

H5427.strongs_def: mineral potash (so called from effervescing with acid)

H5428.kjv_def: destroy, forsake, pluck (out, up, by the roots), pull up, root out (up), [idiom] utterly. s

H5429.derivation: from an unused root meaning to define;
H5429.strongs_def: a seah, or certain measure (as determinative) forgrain

H5430.strongs_def: perhaps a military boot (as aprotection from mud

H5431.derivation: a primitive root; to be miry; used only as denominative from H5430 (סְאוֹן);
H5431.strongs_def: to shoe, i.e. (active participle) a soldier shod

H5433.strongs_def: to quaff to satiety, i.e. become tipsy

H5434.strongs_def: Seba, a son of Cush, and the country settled by him
H5434.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H5436.strongs_def: a Sebaite, or inhabitant of Seba

H5437.strongs_def: to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively
H5437.kjv_def: bring, cast, fetch, lead, make, walk, [idiom] whirl, [idiom] round about, be about on every side, apply, avoid, beset (about), besiege, bring again, carry (about), change, cause to come about, [idiom] circuit, (fetch a) compass (about, round), drive, environ, [idiom] on every side, beset (close, come, compass, go, stand) round about, inclose, remove, return, set, sit down, turn (self) (about, aside, away, back).
H5437.usage: compass(41x), turn(34x), remove(3x), returned(2x), round(2x), side(2x), beset(2x), driven(2x)

H5439.strongs_def: (as noun) a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly (as adverb, with or without preposition) around
H5439.kjv_def: (place, round) about, circuit, compass, on every side.

H5443.derivation: (Aramaic) or שַׂבְּכָא; (Aramaic), from a root corresponding to H5440 (סָבַךְ);

H5445.strongs_def: to carry (literally or figuratively), or (reflexively) be burdensome; specifically, to be gravid
H5445.kjv_def: bear, be a burden, carry, strong to labour.

H5448.derivation: (only in the form סֻבָּל; from H5445 (סָבַל);

H5452.strongs_def: to bear in mind, i.e. hope

H5453.derivation: dual from a root corresponding to H5452 (סְבַר); double hope;

H5454.derivation: or סַבְתָּה; probably of foreign derivation;
H5454.strongs_def: Sabta or Sabtah, the name of a son of Cush, and the country occupied by his posterity

H5455.strongs_def: Sabteca, the name of a son of Cush, and the region settled by him

H5458.strongs_def: properly, shut up, i.e. the breast (as enclosing the heart); also gold (as generally shut up safely)

H5459.derivation: feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to shut up;

H5461.derivation: from an unused root meaning to superintend;

H5462.strongs_def: to shut up; figuratively, to surrender
H5462.kjv_def: close up, deliver (up), give over (up), inclose, [idiom] pure, repair, shut (in, self, out, up, up together), stop, [idiom] straitly.

H5464.derivation: probably from H5462 (סָגַר) in the sense of sweeping away;

H5465.derivation: from an unused root meaning to estop;

H5466.derivation: from an unused root meaning to envelop;

H5467.derivation: from an unused root meaning to scorch; burnt (i.e. volcanic or bituminous) district;
H5467.strongs_def: Sedom, a place near the Dead Sea

H5468.derivation: from an unused root meaning to arrange;

H5469.derivation: from an unused root meaning to be round;

H5472.strongs_def: properly, to flinch, i.e. (by implication) to go back, literally (to retreat) or figuratively (to apostatize)
H5472.kjv_def: backslider, drive, go back, turn (away, back).

H5473.derivation: a primitive root (probably rather identical with H5472 (סוּג) through the idea of shrinking from a hedge; compare H7735 (שׂוּג));

H5474.strongs_def: an inclosure, i.e. cage (for an animal)

H5475.strongs_def: a session, i.e. company of persons (in close deliberation); by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret

H5477.derivation: from an unused root meaning to wipe away; sweeping;

H5480.strongs_def: properly, to smear over (with oil), i.e. anoint

H5482.derivation: (rather to be written סְוֵנָה; for סְוֵן; i.e to Seven); of Egyptian derivation;
H5482.strongs_def: Seven, a place in Upper Egypt

H5483.derivation: or סֻס; from an unused root meaning to skip (properly, for joy);
H5483.strongs_def: a horse (as leaping); also a swallow (from its rapid flight)

H5486.kjv_def: consume, have an end, perish, [idiom] be utterly.

H5492.kjv_def: Red Sea, storm, tempest, whirlwind, Red sea.

H5493.usage: depart(76x), remove(35x), aside(29x), take(14x), turn(12x), go(3x), put(3x), eschewed(3x)

H5495.strongs_def: Sur, a gate of the temple
